data.title: US Defense Secretary: The goal is to destroy Iranian missiles, drones, and the navy; securing the open Strait of Hormuz was not listed as a military objective.
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Hergsays stated that the Pentagon's goal is to destroy Iran's missiles, drones, and navy. He did not list securing the Strait of Hormuz as a military objective, saying it was the task of other countries. Hergsays said, "The Strait of Hormuz issue...is not just an American issue." This statement echoed calls from President Trump, who has urged other countries to address the strategic waterway blocked by Iran. Hergsays stated that a "top priority" is reaching an agreement with Iran, but he did not specify what such an agreement would include. (Jinshi)

data.title: Warren Buffett Sees Limited Opportunities in Current Stock Market
data.description:
Warren Buffett, known as the 'Oracle of Omaha,' expressed skepticism about the attractiveness of current stock market valuations during an interview with CNBC. According to PANews, Buffett noted that despite this year's market downturn, Berkshire Hathaway has not identified many appealing investment opportunities. He downplayed recent market fluctuations, stating that the current environment is far from those that have historically presented significant buying opportunities. Buffett remarked, "Since I took over, the market has dropped more than 50% at least three times. The current situation is not exciting at all."

At 95, Buffett remains actively involved in investment decisions, even after stepping down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway. He continues to work daily, closely monitoring market trends. His routine includes calling Berkshire's financial asset manager, Mark Millard, before the market opens to discuss market dynamics. Millard executes trades based on these discussions, indicating that although Buffett plans to hand over the CEO role to Abel in early 2026, he remains personally engaged in the company's investment activities.

data.title: Drone Strikes Impact Russia's Baltic Oil Ports, Affecting Oil Flows
data.description: Drone strikes on Russia's Baltic oil ports have significantly disrupted the nation's weekly oil flows, Bloomberg posted on X. This development poses a challenge to the Kremlin's financial resources as it aims to benefit from the ongoing conflict in Iran. The strikes have led to a noticeable decline in oil exports, impacting Russia's ability to generate revenue from its energy sector. The situation underscores the vulnerability of critical infrastructure in conflict zones and highlights the broader geopolitical tensions affecting global energy markets.

data.title: Buffett Warns of Increased Challenges if Iran Possesses Nuclear Weapons
data.description: Warren Buffett has expressed concerns over the potential consequences if Iran were to acquire nuclear weapons. According to PANews, Buffett stated that the situation would become more difficult compared to when Iran does not possess such weapons. His remarks highlight the geopolitical implications and increased tensions that could arise from nuclear proliferation in the region.

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Prices Projected to Rise by 2026
data.description: A recent Reuters survey indicates that the average price of U.S. crude oil is expected to reach $76.78 per barrel by 2026. According to BlockBeats, this marks an increase from the February forecast of $60.38 per barrel. Similarly, Brent crude oil is projected to average $82.85 per barrel in 2026, up from the previous estimate of $63.85 per barrel.

data.title: Expert Highlights Atomic Settlement's Impact on Capital Efficiency
data.description: Chris Kim has emphasized that atomic settlement can eliminate counterparty risk, though it may impact capital efficiency by necessitating real-time funding for each trade. According to NS3.AI, Kim noted that stablecoin transfer volume has surpassed $1.8 trillion. However, he cautioned that quicker settlement processes might lead to wider spreads and diminished market depth for retail investors.

data.title: Buffett: Stock market valuations are not attractive, but he will remain deeply involved in investment decisions.
data.description: In an interview with CNBC, Warren Buffett stated that stock market valuations remain unattractive, and Berkshire Hathaway hasn't found many buyable targets in the market after this year's stock market decline. Buffett downplayed recent market volatility, saying the current environment is far from creating significant buying opportunities in the past: "Since I took over, the market has fallen by more than 50% at least three times. There's nothing to get excited about right now." Even after stepping down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, the 95-year-old Buffett remains deeply involved in investment decisions. He said he still goes to the office daily to continuously monitor the market. His routine includes calling Mark Millard, head of Berkshire's financial assets, before the market opens to discuss market dynamics. Millard executes trades based on these discussions, indicating that although Buffett will hand over the CEO position to Abel in early 2026, he remains personally involved. (Jinshi)

data.title: Coinbase: EDGEX spot trading will launch on March 31, 2026.
data.description: Coinbase Markets announced on its X platform that EDGEX (EDGEX) spot trading will launch on March 31, 2026. If liquidity conditions are met, the EDGEX-USD trading pair will open later today in supported regions.

data.title: CZ: Cryptocurrencies can cope with the impact of quantum computing simply by upgrading to quantum-resistant algorithms.
data.description: CZ stated in an article on the X platform that cryptocurrencies only need to upgrade to quantum-resistant (post-quantum) algorithms to cope with the effects of quantum computing, and there is no need to panic. In practical terms, upgrading organizations face difficulties in decentralized environments, and the choice of algorithm may lead to disputes and forks. Some projects that have ceased development may not upgrade, which helps to clean up such projects. New code may introduce vulnerabilities or security issues in the short term, and users who hold their cryptocurrencies themselves should migrate their assets to new wallets. Regarding Satoshi's Bitcoin holdings, if the assets have moved, it means they are still active; if they have not moved within a certain period, the relevant addresses should be locked or destroyed to prevent hacking. Fundamentally, encryption is easier than decryption, and stronger computing power is a positive factor; cryptocurrencies will continue to exist after the quantum era.

data.title: Iranian Military Advisor Killed in U.S.-Israel Joint Attack
data.description: An Iranian military advisor, Jamshid Ehaki, was killed in a joint attack by the United States and Israel. According to PANews, the incident was reported by Xinhua News Agency on March 31. The attack highlights ongoing tensions in the region, with implications for international relations and security dynamics.

data.title: Trump Claims Victory Announcement Imminent
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated on March 31 that a victory announcement is imminent. According to BlockBeats, he also remarked that removing Iran's uranium mines would be 'very difficult.'

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth Discusses Options for Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth stated on March 31 that alternative options are available if the Hormuz Strait remains closed. According to BlockBeats, he emphasized that the world should be prepared for an escalation regarding the situation in the Hormuz Strait.

data.title: The Better Money Company Secures $10 Million Seed Funding for Stablecoin Clearinghouse
data.description:
Former a16z crypto investor Sam Broner has announced the establishment of The Better Money Company with university friend Adam Zuckerman. According to Odaily, the company has successfully completed a $10 million seed funding round, led by a16z crypto, with participation from BoxGroup and Sunflower Capital. Angel investors, including Circle co-founder Sean Neville and former Microsoft executive Charlie Songhurst, also contributed.

The Better Money Company aims to create a stablecoin clearinghouse, offering clients low-cost services for exchanging various dollar-pegged tokens. Broner, who spent over two years at a16z crypto focusing on stablecoin investments, and Zuckerman, who previously worked at Latham & Watkins and served as general counsel at Eigen Labs, founded the company in November last year.

The company has secured commitments from issuers such as Paxos, Stripe's Bridge, and MoonPay, and plans to support all tokens compliant with the enacted Genius Act, excluding USDT. The U.S. version, USAT, remains unaffected. While the product is not yet publicly available, it is expected to be accessible to clients in the coming weeks.

data.title: Former A16Z crypto investor raises $10 million to establish stablecoin clearinghouse Better Money
data.description: Former a16z crypto investor Sam Broner announced the launch of The Better Money Company with his college friend Adam Zuckerman, raising $10 million in seed funding. The round was led by a16z crypto, with participation from BoxGroup and Sunflower Capital, and angel investors including Circle co-founder Sean Neville and former Microsoft executive Charlie Songhurst. The company aims to establish a stablecoin clearinghouse, providing customers with low-cost exchange services for various USD-pegged tokens. Broner previously worked at a16z crypto for over two years, focusing on stablecoin investments. Zuckerman previously worked at Latham & Watkins and later served as general counsel for Eigen Labs. The two founded the company last November and have already secured commitments from several issuers, including Paxos, Stripe's Bridge, and MoonPay. The company plans to support all tokens compliant with the signed and effective Genius Act, excluding USDT (its US version, USAT, is unaffected). The product is not yet publicly available but is expected to be available to customers in the coming weeks.

data.title: US Defense Secretary Herbert: (Regarding the Strait of Hormuz) The world should prepare for escalation.
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Hergsays: (Regarding the Strait of Hormuz) The world should prepare for an escalation. (Jinshi)

data.title: King Charles III to Visit U.S. Amid Tensions Between Trump and Starmer
data.description: King Charles III is set to embark on a state visit to the United States in late April, coinciding with heightened tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Bloomberg posted on X that the announcement of the visit came shortly after President Trump reiterated his criticisms of the United Kingdom. The timing of the visit underscores the strained relations between the two leaders, as diplomatic engagements continue amidst political discord.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Kazakhstan's Gold Reserves Rise to 11.17 Million Ounces in February
data.description: Kazakhstan's gold reserves increased to 11.17 million ounces in February, according to Jin10. This marks a significant rise in the country's precious metal holdings, reflecting strategic efforts to bolster economic stability through asset diversification. The increase in gold reserves is part of Kazakhstan's broader economic strategy to enhance financial security amid global market fluctuations. The move aligns with similar actions by other nations seeking to strengthen their economic resilience through precious metal investments.

data.title: U.S. Military Targets Iranian Mine-Laying Capabilities
data.description: The U.S. military is focusing on Iran's mine-laying capabilities, according to BlockBeats. On March 31, U.S. Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Dan Kane, announced that the first B-52 bombers have commenced land-based missions aimed at addressing this threat.

data.title: Bitcoin developers are developing quantum-resistant Bitcoin improvement proposals and have already deployed testnets.
data.description: Bitcoin Magazine published an article on the X platform stating that developers are currently working on a Bitcoin improvement proposal to enhance Bitcoin's ability to resist quantum attacks, and the relevant testnet has been deployed.

data.title: Illinois Senator Proposes Bill for Public Funds Investment in Bitcoin
data.description: Illinois State Senator Steve McClure has introduced Bill SB3672, known as the Local Government Digital Asset Investment Act, which aims to amend the state's Public Funds Investment Act to allow public institutions to allocate up to 5% of public funds into Bitcoin-related financial products. According to ChainCatcher, the bill defines eligible 'digital asset stocks' as equity in publicly traded companies that, according to their latest audited financial statements, hold at least 25% of their total assets in Bitcoin or other digital currencies. This threshold includes companies like Strategy, Twenty One Capital, Metaplanet, and Semler Scientific, which focus on Bitcoin accumulation as a core strategy. The bill also permits public funds to invest in Bitcoin ETFs and Bitcoin bonds, with additional transparency requirements.
